name: Application Whitelisting Bypass via Bginfo Execution
description: Detects suspicious execution of `bginfo.exe`, a legitimate Sysinternals tool, with command-line arguments that enable automated or silent execution of embedded VBScript. Adversaries can abuse `bginfo.exe` as a signed binary proxy to bypass application whitelisting and achieve code execution.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{computer_name}**: the process `bginfo.exe` executed the command line **{command_line}**, which contains patterns indicative of **Signed Binary Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1218**). This technique is a known method for bypassing application whitelisting and executing arbitrary code. The parent process was **{parent_image}** and the user was **{user}**. While `bginfo.exe` is a legitimate administrative tool, its use for automated or silent execution of scripts is highly suspicious. Immediate investigation is required to determine the nature of the script being executed and whether this is a legitimate administrative activity or a malicious attack.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line that includes arguments for bginfo.exe.

parameters.name: parent_image
parameters.description: The parent process that launched bginfo.exe.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process ran.

parameters.name: computer_name
parameters.description: The name of the computer where the event occurred.
